content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
September 14, 1989 
Lance H. Olson 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Olson: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-499 
This letter confirms the telephone advice I provided to you 
on August 17, 1989, concerning the fundraising activities of the 
Committee to Retain Assemblyman Jack O'Connell. 
You have informed 
us that Assemblyman O'Connell has been served with notice of 
intent to circulate recall petitions, pursuant to Elections Code 
Sections 27020-27022. 
Assemblyman O'Connell has formed the Com-
mittee to Retain Assemblyman Jack O'Connell to raise money to 
defend against the recall effort. 
We have issued advice that (1) contributions to support or 
oppose a recall effort are similar to contributions in a ballot 
measure election and, therefore are not subject to the contribu-
tion limitations of the Political Reform Act (the Act),l (2) 
elected officers may establish a separate controlled committee to 
defend against a recall effort, and (3) an elected officer's 
recall defense committee may receive contributions in unlimited 
amounts, to be used only to defend against the recall effort, 
after the elected officer has been served with notice of the 
recall effort pursuant to Elections Code sections 27020-27022. 
(Roberti Advice Letter, No. A-89-358.) 
You have asked whether 
this advice would change if provisions of Proposition 68 take ef-
fect as a result of the recent court of appeal decision in Taxpay-
ers to Limit Campaign Spending v. FPPC. 
In our telephone conversation, I advised you that our advice 
would not change if the provisions of Proposition 68 take effect 
1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


File No. A-89-499 
Page 2 
because Proposition 68's contribution limitations do not apply to 
ballot measure elections. 
Accordingly, notwithstanding section 
85309 of Proposition 68, the committee to Retain Assemblyman Jack 
O'Connell could continue to accept contributions during a non-
election year. 
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:plh 
Sincerely,

Kathryn~. Donovan 
General Counsel 


LANCE H. OLSON 
BRUCE J. HAGEL 
LEROY Y. FONG 
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH 
OF COUNSEL 
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member 
California State Legislature 
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350 
Sacramento, California 95814 
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952 
FAX: (916) 442-1280 
Law Offices of 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG ,..,,,"t 
August 18, 1989 
Ms. Kathryn Donovan, General Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J street, suite 800 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
RE: 
contirmation ot Advice 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
AUG 223 15 PH 11 
The purpose of this letter is to confirm our 
conversation of August 17, 1989 concerning Assemblyman 
Jack O'Connell. 
During the conversation I advised you 
that I represent Assemblyman Jack O'Connell and I serve 
as Treasurer for a Committee he has formed for the 
purposes of opposing a recall effort underway in his 
Assembly District against him. 
The name of the 
Committee is "Committee to Retain Assemblyman Jack 
O'Connell". 
In our conversation I stated that Mr. O'Connell 
desired to continue to raise contributions for his 
recall Committee when and if the provisions of 
Proposition 68 were to go into effect as a result of 
the recent decision in Taxpayers To Limit campaign 
Spending vs. FPPC. 
You indicated that if and when the provisions of 
Proposition 68 as determined by the Court in the 
Taxpayer's lawsuit were to go into effect they would 
not prohibit Assemblyman O'Connell from continuing to 
raise campaign contributions during this "non-election 
year" . 
I would appreciate your written confirmation of 
this advice at your earliest convenience. 
Thank you 
for your attention in this matter. 
Very truly yours, 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
LANCE H. OLSON

Dear Ms. Donovan: 
AUG

conversation of August 17, 1989 concerning Assemblyman

as Treasurer for a Committee he has formed for the 
purposes of opposing a recall effort underway in his 
Assembly District against him. 
The name of the

O'Connell". 
In our conversation I stated that Mr. O'Connell 
desired to continue to raise contributions for his 
recall Committee when and if the provisions of 
proposition 68 were to go into effect as a result of 
the recent decision in Taxpayers To Limit Campaign 
Spending vs. FPPC. 
You indicated that if and when the provisions of 
Proposition 68 as determined by the Court in the 
Taxpayer's lawsuit were to go into effect they would 
not prohibit Assemblyman O'Connell from continuing to 
raise campaign contributions during this "non-election 
year". 
I would appreciate your written confirmation of

Thank you 
for your attention in this matter. 
Very truly yours, 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
LANCE H. OLSON 
cc: 
Assemblyman Jack O'Connell 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
August 28, 1989 
Lance Olson 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, suite 350 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-499 
Dear Mr. Olson: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on August 22, 1989. 
If you have 
any questions, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely,

sections.conclusion: We have issued advice that (1) contributions to support or oppose a recall effort are similar to contributions in a ballot measure election and, therefore are not subject to the contribution limitations of the Political Reform Act (the Act), (2) elected officers may establish a separate controlled committee to defend against a recall effort, and (3) an elected officer's recall defense committee may receive contributions in unlimited amounts, to be used only to defend against the recall effort, after the elected officer has been served with notice of the recall effort pursuant to Elections Code sections 27020-27022. Our advice would not change if the provisions of Proposition 68 take effect because Proposition 68's contribution limitations do not apply to ballot measure elections. Accordingly, notwithstanding section 85309 of Proposition 68, the committee to Retain Assemblyman Jack O'Connell could continue to accept contributions during a non-election year.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether FPPC advice regarding unlimited contributions to a recall defense committee would change if Proposition 68's contribution limitations take effect, and whether such a committee can continue accepting contributions during a non-election year.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirmed that recall defense committees are treated like ballot measure committees and are exempt from contribution limitations under both the Political Reform Act and Proposition 68. Therefore, the Committee to Retain Assemblyman Jack O'Connell may continue accepting unlimited contributions during the non-election year.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes telephone advice confirmation (dated September 14, 1989) and related incoming requests/correspondence from counsel. Primary FPPC response is the September 14 letter. OCR quality is good. All substantive conclusion text extracted verbatim.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether FPPC advice regarding unlimited contributions to a recall defense committee would change if Proposition 68's contribution limitations take effect, and whether such a committee can continue accepting contributions during a non-election year.

CONCLUSION: We have issued advice that (1) contributions to support or oppose a recall effort are similar to contributions in a ballot measure election and, therefore are not subject to the contribution limitations of the Political Reform Act (the Act), (2) elected officers may establish a separate controlled committee to defend against a recall effort, and (3) an elected officer's recall defense committee may receive contributions in unlimited amounts, to be used only to defend against the recall effort, after the elected officer has been served with notice of the recall effort pursuant to Elections Code sections 27020-27022. Our advice would not change if the provisions of Proposition 68 take effect because Proposition 68's contribution limitations do not apply to ballot measure elections. Accordingly, notwithstanding section 85309 of Proposition 68, the committee to Retain Assemblyman Jack O'Connell could continue to accept contributions during a non-election year.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that a recall defense committee for Assemblyman Jack O'Connell can accept unlimited contributions, and that this position would not change if Proposition 68 takes effect, since ballot measure elections are exempt from Proposition 68's contribution limitations.
